title: Broad SMA Cross Trend Guard Program
objective: Run one narrower, longer-horizon SMA-cross branch that forces benchmark-bucket balance before the family is retired for good.
branch_rationale: The fast reentry and slower confirmation SMA-cross branches tested speed and patience, but they did not test whether the family needs fewer names and stricter bucket balance at the same time. This branch is the bounded confirmation of that hypothesis.
positive_hypotheses: A 35/180 crossover can keep the trend thesis slower than reentry while still reacting sooner than the 50/150 confirmation branch.
Limiting the basket to five names with one benchmark-bucket slot can reduce the benchmark-relative fragility that hurt the earlier SMA-cross branches.

seed_stack.label: SMA-cross confirmation control
seed_stack.rationale: Reference control for the slower SMA-cross confirmation branch.

seed_stack.label: Trend-guard seed
seed_stack.rationale: Repo-backed trend-guard branch using a narrower basket and explicit benchmark-bucket balance.

campaign_path.label: SMA-Cross Trend-Guard Seed
campaign_path.purpose: Execute the guarded SMA-cross family and decide whether it should be promoted or retired.

stop_conditions.when: The trend-guard seed passes validation, holdout, and walk-forward promotion rules under the current policy.
The seed records a terminal promotion decision and still fails the policy, especially on benchmark-relative split or walk-forward evidence.
